yaml.title: Dr Timothy L. Molloy
yaml.subtitle: Senior Lecturer · Department of Electrical and Computer Systems Engineering · Monash University

markdown.markdown:
<!-- Add a profile photo by placing an image file (e.g., profile.jpg) in this directory
     and uncommenting the lines below:

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="25%"}
![](profile.jpg){.rounded-circle width=200}
:::
::: {.column width="75%"}
-->

Engineering academic with research experience and interests spanning industry and academia, including
topics in Robotics, Control, Signal Processing, AI, Machine Learning, and Information Theory.
I have collaborated with industry, government, and academic institutions including Boeing,
Defence Science and Technology Group (Australia), Karlsruhe Institute of Technology (Germany),
Boston University (USA), and UT Dallas (USA).

I strive to inspire and empower people through education and research, with success in lecturing
and industry engagement evidenced by winning a Boeing Wirraway Team Award, being awarded a
competitive Advance Queensland Research Fellowship, and receiving high instructor and course
ratings teaching Robotics, Control, Engineering Data Analytics, and Statistical Inference in Mechatronics.

<!-- :::
:::: -->

---


### Senior Lecturer — Monash University {.mt-4}
**2026 –**  |  Department of Electrical and Computer Systems Engineering

Continuing academic appointment with 40% Research, 40% Teaching, and 20% Service commitments.

---

### Senior Lecturer — The Australian National University {.mt-4}
**2022 – 2026**  |  School of Engineering (Mechatronics)

Continuing academic appointment with 40% Research, 40% Teaching, and 20% Service commitments.

Selected highlights:

- Research spanning inverse optimal control using systems and control theory, machine learning, and
  optimisation; multiagent dynamic and differential game theory; and statistical signal processing and
  information theory for event-based robot perception.
- Taught engineering and computing students at both undergraduate and postgraduate levels in Robotics,
  Engineering Data Analytics, and Statistical Inference in Mechatronics.
- Served as Inaugural Engineering Fundamentals Discipline Chair overseeing first- and second-year
  fundamentals courses in ANU's Bachelor of Engineering (Honours).

---

### Research Fellow — The University of Melbourne {.mt-4}
**2020 – 2022**  |  Australia–US Multidisciplinary University Research Initiative (AUSMURI)

Project: *Neuro-Autonomy: Neuro-inspired perception, navigation and spatial awareness for autonomous robots.*

Selected highlights:

- Found new solutions to partially observed Markov decision processes (POMDPs) previously considered
  intractable in robotics and AI — applied to autonomous robot exploration, privacy, and cybersecurity.
- Collaborated with ARC Laureate Fellow Prof Michael Milford and IEEE Fellow Prof Girish Nair on a
  principled Bayesian approach to information fusion for robot vision-based localisation.

---

### Advance Queensland Research Fellow — QUT & Boeing Research & Technology {.mt-4}
**2017 – 2019**  |  Competitive Advance Queensland Early-Career Industry Research Fellowship

Selected highlights:

- Embedded part-time (0.5 FTE) with the Autonomous Systems Team of Boeing Research & Technology.
- Developed a new optimal bearing-only aircraft collision avoidance approach that has *transitioned
  into operation* at Boeing, published in the AIAA Journal of Guidance, Control, and Dynamics.

---

### Research Associate — Queensland University of Technology {.mt-4}
**2015 – 2017**  |  ARC Linkage Project and Direct-Funded Industry Projects

Selected highlights:

- Created novel inverse dynamic game and inverse optimal control approaches for modelling the
  collision-avoidance behaviours of birds, informing strategies for autonomous aircraft.
- Collaborated with mathematicians from Boeing Research & Technology – Australia and biologists
  from the Queensland Brain Institute, University of Queensland.
- Co-led direct-funded industry projects developing novel robot vision and signal processing
  algorithms for vision-based aircraft detection and tracking for Boeing Research & Technology – Australia.

---

## Education

**Doctor of Philosophy** (2011 – 2015)  
Queensland University of Technology (QUT), Australia  
*Optimal and robust detection and estimation in stochastic dynamical systems with application to
robotics and autonomous systems.*  
🏆 QUT Outstanding Doctoral Thesis Award

**Bachelor of Engineering (Aerospace Avionics) with First Class Honours** (2007 – 2010)  
Queensland University of Technology (QUT), Australia · GPA: 6.969/7  
🏆 QUT University Medal

---

## Awards

| Year | Award |
|------|-------|
| 2018 | Boeing Wirraway Award — Team award with colleagues from Boeing Research & Technology – Australia's Autonomous Systems Team and QUT |
| 2017 | Advance Queensland Early-Career Research Fellowship — Queensland State Government |
| 2016 | Outstanding Doctoral Thesis Award — Queensland University of Technology (top 5% annually) |
| 2013 | High Commendation, Best Student Paper — Engineers Australia |
| 2011 | Australian Postgraduate Award — Queensland University of Technology |
| 2010 | University Medal — Queensland University of Technology |
| 2010 | JH Curtis Award — Engineers Australia (Queensland Division) |
| 2010 | DSTO Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance Division Undergraduate Prize |
| 2006 | Australian Students Prize — Australian Government (top 500 high school students nationally) |

---

## Contact

| | |
|---|---|
| **Email** | [timothy.molloy@anu.edu.au](mailto:timothy.molloy@anu.edu.au) |
| **LinkedIn** | [au.linkedin.com/in/tlmolloy](https://au.linkedin.com/in/tlmolloy) |
| **Google Scholar** | [scholar.google.com](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=GX6_KKYAAAAJ) |
